title: Soldier Accused of Using Secret Information to Win Money
summary: A U.S. soldier named Gannon Ken Van Dyke has been charged with using secret information to win more than $400,000 in online bets. The soldier was part of a mission to capture the president of Venezuela. He used classified information about the mission to place bets on a website called Polymarket. The bets were about whether U.S. forces would be in Venezuela and whether the president would be removed by a certain date. After winning the money, he tried to hide it by moving it to a cryptocurrency account and asked the betting website to delete his account. The FBI director said that no one is above the law and that people with security clearances cannot use secret information for personal gain. The soldier could face years in prison.

keywords.explanation: secret information that only certain people are allowed to know
a guess about what will happen, where you can win money if you are right
a special job or task, often for the military
a type of digital money that exists only on computers

questions.question: What did the soldier do wrong?
options: He used secret information to win money
He went on vacation without permission
He forgot to do his job

What website did he use to bet?
What did the soldier do with the money after winning?
options: He moved it to a cryptocurrency account
He spent it on a car

questions.question: Who said no one is above the law?
What could happen to the soldier?

background_read: The U.S. military sometimes carries out missions in other countries. Soldiers must follow rules and keep secrets safe. Using secret information for personal gain is illegal.
Article_Structure: WHO: A U.S. soldier named Gannon Ken Van Dyke
WHAT: He used secret information to win money in online bets
WHERE: The bets were placed on a website called Polymarket
WHY: He wanted to make money for himself

perspectives.description: The government says that using secret information for personal gain is wrong and against the law.
The soldier thought he could use secret information to win money without getting caught.
